courses.title: Redstone Basics
realWorldConnections: Redstone circuits teach boolean logic — the same logic that runs every computer
Signal strength decreasing over distance is like voltage drop in electrical wiring
Logic gates (AND, OR, NOT) are how CPUs make decisions — Minecraft is literally a computer
Pistons demonstrate mechanical advantage and energy conversion
Repeaters are like signal boosters in fiber optic cables

courses.title: Building Basics
realWorldConnections: Block placement teaches spatial reasoning and 3D thinking
Roof angles relate to real architecture — steep roofs shed snow and rain
Bridge construction involves the same engineering principles as real bridges
Garden design connects to landscape architecture and urban planning
Material mixing teaches color theory and visual design

courses.title: Survival 101
realWorldConnections: Crafting recipes teach logical sequencing and resource management
Shelter building connects to real survival skills and architecture
Mob combat teaches situational awareness and risk assessment
Farming teaches patience, planning, and sustainable resource management
Light level calculations are essentially math applied to safety

courses.title: Advanced Redstone
realWorldConnections: Flip-flops are actual computer memory cells — you're building RAM
Clock circuits pulse like heartbeats in electronics
Pulse logic is used in real digital circuits for timing and synchronization

courses.title: Redstone Computers
realWorldConnections: You will literally build a working computer — this is how real CPUs work
Binary arithmetic is the foundation of all modern computing
Instruction sets are how software talks to hardware

paths.title: Redstone Engineer
paths.path: From torch to computer — master redstone engineering
paths.description: Complete path from basic redstone to building your own computer

paths.title: Master Builder
paths.path: From dirt hut to architectural marvel

paths.title: Survival Expert
paths.description: Master survival skills and add redstone automation
